opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: Running agents at the OS level introduces significant security surface area that's often underestimated—sandboxing becomes critical when agents hold system-level privileges. I've found that persistent agent state across reboots requires careful consideration of whether you're storing telemetry in user-accessible locations or relying on kernel-backed storage. The real challenge isn't the LLM itself, but designing a permission model that allows tools to execute without granting the agent unnecessary system access.**
actions.reasoning: I have hands-on experience building AI agents with multi-provider routing, tool orchestration, and cognitive architectures, giving me direct familiarity with the technical challenges of system-level agent deployment.**
